text: I don't drink. I don't drink. Just not appealing to me.
Hi, good morning, everybody. I just wanted to share a news article that I ran upon here.
Kind of a hot topic in America. They say less and less Americans are drinking alcohol.
Usually, you hear of increases, not decreases in alcohol consumption. So, that kind of caught my
attention. But big statistic here, they say only 54% of US adults are drinking alcohol.
And that's the lowest number in decades. Alcohol has been such a big part of American culture.
Personally, I grew up around alcohol. There's fires everywhere. There's party stores everywhere.
It's just interesting to see that alcohol consumption is slowly declining.
The younger generation seems to be more health conscious, which is very good.
And they're saying that it's possible, too, now that marijuana in America, I don't want to say
is legal, but in half of the states in America is legalized. So, our younger adults preferring
marijuana over alcohol. Another factor is changing social habits among young people.
Since alcohol is often used socially, less in-person time with friends may mean less drinking.
Monthly in-person social time dropped from 30 hours in 2003 to just 10 hours in the year 2020,
especially for ages 15 to 24, a decline made worse by the COVID-19 pandemic. On top of that,
mocktails and non-alcoholic options like beer, wine, and liquor are becoming more popular,
giving people other choices when they do get together. If you're celebrating, you have drinks,
it's been a part of my life. I have one or two drinks here and there, and I don't feel it affects me,
you know, in a negative way, health wise or anything like that. Everybody has to do what's right for
them personally. That's my own little thought on it. If you start becoming dependent on it,
if it's starting to rule your life for a little bit, then you're drinking way too much. You guys
have a great day or a great evening. Bye-bye.
